testcase.name: arrow-navigation › 31. Arrow Navigation / Between paragraphs :: Arrow Down at block end focuses next paragraph
arrow-navigation › 31. Arrow Navigation / Between paragraphs :: Arrow Up at block start focuses previous paragraph
arrow-navigation › 31. Arrow Navigation / Divider navigation :: focused divider uses Arrow Up and Arrow Down to jump to adjacent blocks
arrow-navigation › 31. Arrow Navigation / Table entry :: Arrow Down from paragraph end enters first table cell

testcase.name: arrow-navigation › 31. Arrow Navigation / Code blocks :: Arrow Up at top of code textarea exits to previous block
arrow-navigation › 31. Arrow Navigation / Code blocks :: Arrow Down at bottom of code textarea exits to next block
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: Add icon assigns a page icon when the page starts without one
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: clicking the page icon opens the asset picker and Escape closes it
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: selecting an asset updates the page icon immediately
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: clicking outside the page icon picker closes it
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: the page icon picker can be reopened after closing it once
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: Remove icon clears the page icon and restores the Add icon action
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page icons :: clicking a custom page icon reopens its picker after the icon has already been changed
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: new callout blocks start with the default light bulb icon
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: clicking a callout icon opens its picker and Escape closes it
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: clicking outside the callout icon picker closes it
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: selecting an asset updates the callout icon immediately
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: the callout icon picker can be reopened after being closed
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: clicking a custom callout icon reopens its picker after the icon has already changed
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Callout icons :: Remove icon resets the callout icon back to the default light bulb
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: Add cover assigns a cover to a page that starts without one
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: selecting a cover from the picker updates the page cover immediately
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: clicking Change cover opens the cover picker and outside click closes it
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: pressing Escape closes the cover picker
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: the cover picker can be reopened after it has been closed
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: closing the cover picker without selecting anything keeps the current cover in place
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Page cover :: Remove cover clears the current cover and restores Add cover
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: creating an image from an empty paragraph converts the current block without leaving an extra paragraph behind
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: an image block can be created from the slash menu on an empty paragraph
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: a video block can be created from the slash menu on an empty paragraph
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: an audio block can be created from the slash menu on an empty paragraph
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: a file block can be created from the slash menu on an empty paragraph
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: selecting media in a paragraph with existing text inserts a new media block below
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: changing an image asset updates the image preview without changing block type
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: pressing Escape closes a media picker without changing the current block
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: clicking outside a media picker closes it without changing the current block
assets › 12. Emojis, Icons & Media / Media blocks :: the media caption remains inline-editable and still opens the formatting toolbar on selection
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: typing / in an empty paragraph opens the slash menu
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: typing /hea filters the slash menu down to heading options
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: pressing Enter on /quote converts the block without leaving extra slash text behind
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: clicking Bulleted List converts the current block and cleans the slash text
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: backspacing past the slash trigger closes the slash menu
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: pressing Escape closes the slash menu and keeps the typed slash text
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Slash menu :: the slash menu can be reopened normally after a previous cancelled use
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing # and a space turns the paragraph into a heading
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing - and a space turns the paragraph into a bulleted list item
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing [] and a space turns the paragraph into a to-do item
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing [ ] and a space also turns the paragraph into a to-do item
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing 1. and a space turns the paragraph into a numbered list item
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing --- converts the paragraph into a divider
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: typing a fenced code marker turns the paragraph into a code block
block-creation › 1. Block Creation & Type Selection / Markdown shortcuts :: markdown shortcuts remove the typed prefix instead of keeping it in the final content

testcase.name: category-registry › 27. Block Category Registry / NON_INDENTABLE :: blocks marked as non-indentable keep Tab behavior local instead of structural
category-registry › 27. Block Category Registry / enterCreatesChild :: blocks marked enterCreatesChild create nested children on Enter
category-registry › 27. Block Category Registry / sibling enter behavior :: blocks not marked enterCreatesChild create siblings rather than nested children

containers-and-paste › 7. Toggle Block / Basic toggle behavior :: clicking the chevron collapses and re-expands toggle children without losing them

testcase.name: containers-and-paste › 7. Toggle Block / Toggle children as full blocks :: Tab indents one toggle child under another inside the toggle tree
containers-and-paste › 7. Toggle Block / Toggle + external interactions :: a root block can be moved into a collapsed toggle and remains hidden until the toggle is expanded
containers-and-paste › 7. Toggle Block / Toggle + external interactions :: deleting an empty toggle summary promotes its child blocks to the parent level
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Callout :: callout children are rendered inside the callout box
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Callout :: indenting a paragraph under a callout renders it as a child inside the container
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Callout :: slash menu commands work inside callout children
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Callout :: deleting an empty callout keeps nested grandchildren under their promoted parent
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Callout :: deleting an empty callout promotes its children instead of deleting their content
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Quote :: quote children are rendered inside the quote branch
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Quote :: a quote can contain multiple child blocks rendered inside the quote branch
containers-and-paste › 8. Callout & Quote as Containers / Quote :: deleting an empty quote promotes its children according to editor rules

testcase.name: containers-and-paste (serial) › 10. Paste Handling / Paste handling :: pasting a fenced code block creates a code block with the pasted content
containers-and-paste (serial) › 10. Paste Handling / Paste handling :: pasting heading levels 4-6 preserves each heading level style
containers-and-paste (serial) › 10. Paste Handling / Paste handling :: pasting a single short line keeps it as inline text in the current block
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: right-clicking a block opens the block context menu with its main sections
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: clicking the drag handle opens the same block context menu
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Insert text above creates a new paragraph above the current block and focuses it
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Insert text below creates a new paragraph below the current block and focuses it
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Duplicate creates an identical leaf block below the original
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Move up swaps the selected block with the one above it
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Move down swaps the selected block with the one below it
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Move down stays hidden on the last root block
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Turn into Heading 1 transforms the block while preserving its text
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Context menu + children :: Delete removes only the selected leaf block when it has no children
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Context menu + children :: Delete on a parent block removes its whole subtree
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Context menu + children :: Delete on a toggle parent removes the whole toggle subtree
context-menu › 5. Context Menu / Context menu + children :: Duplicate on a parent block duplicates its subtree as independent copies
context-menu (serial) › 5. Context Menu / Basic operations :: Copy text writes the current block text to the clipboard

testcase.name: drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Same-level reorder :: dragging a root block above another root block reorders siblings
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Same-level reorder :: dragging a nested child above another nested child reorders siblings inside the same parent
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Cross-level moves :: dragging a child block below its parent at root level moves it out of the hierarchy
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Cross-level moves :: dragging a child between parent groups moves it into the new parent subtree
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Cross-level moves :: dragging a parent block keeps its nested subtree together
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Safety checks :: starting a drag marks the dragged block with reduced opacity
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Safety checks :: dragging over another block shows a visible drop indicator
drag-and-drop › 6. Drag and Drop / Safety checks :: dropping a block onto itself leaves the order unchanged
duplicate-title-numbering › 34. Duplicate Title Numbering / First duplicate :: duplicating a page appends (1) and preserves the original title
duplicate-title-numbering › 34. Duplicate Title Numbering / Incremental numbering :: each new duplicate increments to the next number instead of reusing earlier titles

testcase.name: duplicate-title-numbering › 34. Duplicate Title Numbering / Subtree duplication :: duplicate numbering applies to the duplicated page while child pages clone under the new parent
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / Standard blocks :: pressing Enter in a paragraph creates a new paragraph below and moves focus to it
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / Standard blocks :: pressing Enter in a heading creates a normal paragraph below
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter in a bulleted list item creates a new bullet below
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter in a numbered list item creates the next number
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter in a to-do item creates a new unchecked to-do below
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter on a checked empty to-do item converts it into a paragraph and clears the checked state
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter on an empty bulleted list item converts it into a paragraph
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter on an empty numbered list item converts it into a paragraph
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / List continuation :: pressing Enter on an empty to-do item converts it into a paragraph
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / Container blocks :: pressing Enter in a callout creates a child paragraph inside the callout
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / Container blocks :: pressing Enter in a quote creates a child paragraph inside the quote container
editing-behavior › 3. Enter Key Behavior / Container blocks :: pressing Enter in a toggle summary expands it and focuses the first child
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Empty block deletion :: Backspace on an empty bulleted list item deletes it and keeps focus on a logical sibling
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Empty block deletion :: Backspace on an empty root paragraph deletes it and focuses the previous block
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Empty block deletion :: Delete on the first empty root paragraph deletes it and focuses the next logical block
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Empty block deletion :: Backspace on an empty heading converts it into a paragraph instead of deleting it
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Empty block deletion :: Backspace on an empty indented paragraph outdents it instead of deleting it
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Divider deletion :: Backspace on a focused divider removes it from the page
editing-behavior › 4. Backspace / Delete Behavior / Divider deletion :: Delete on a focused divider removes it from the page
focus-management › 28. Focus management / Enter transitions :: after Enter on a heading the focus moves into the new paragraph below
focus-management › 28. Focus management / Delete transitions :: after deleting the first empty root block the focus moves to the next logical block
focus-management › 28. Focus management / Container transitions :: pressing Enter on an empty toggle summary moves focus into the newly created child
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Basic indentation :: pressing Tab indents a paragraph under the previous sibling
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Basic indentation :: pressing Shift+Tab outdents an indented paragraph back to root level
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Basic indentation :: repeated Tab builds hierarchy one level at a time instead of skipping directly to deep nesting
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Basic indentation :: Shift+Tab only outdents one level at a time from a deep hierarchy
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Basic indentation :: pressing Tab on the first root block does nothing
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Basic indentation :: pressing Shift+Tab on a root paragraph does nothing
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Type-specific indentation :: a heading can be indented under a paragraph and keeps its heading styling

testcase.name: indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Type-specific indentation :: a paragraph cannot be indented under a code block
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Type-specific indentation :: a paragraph cannot be indented under a divider
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Type-specific indentation :: a paragraph can be indented under a callout container
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Type-specific indentation :: a paragraph can be indented under a quote container
indentation › 2. Indentation (Tab / Shift+Tab) / Type-specific indentation :: pressing Tab inside a code block inserts spaces instead of changing hierarchy
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: selecting visible text opens the inline toolbar
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: selecting text inside a heading also opens the inline toolbar
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: selecting text inside a callout opens the inline toolbar
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: placing only the caret keeps the inline toolbar hidden
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: clearing the selection closes the inline toolbar
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: clicking outside the editor closes the inline toolbar on blur
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Toolbar visibility :: selecting inside a code block uses the native textarea selection only
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: the text color button opens the text color palette
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: the background color button opens the background palette
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: applying text color changes only the selected text
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: applying background color changes only the selected text
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: text color survives additional typing without stacking wrappers
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: background color survives additional typing without stacking wrappers
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: applying the same text color twice removes the text color wrapper
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: applying the same background color twice removes the background wrapper
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: background color excludes trailing whitespace from the highlighted wrapper
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Color actions :: text color and background color can coexist with bold and italic while typing
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: the bold button toggles bold on and off
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: the italic button toggles italic on and off
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: the strikethrough button toggles strikethrough on and off
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: bold and italic can coexist on the same selection
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: the inline code button wraps the selection as inline code
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: inline code keeps inherited custom text and background colors
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Format actions :: mixed inline formats remain stable after typing and deleting inside the same region
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: the Add link button opens the link type chooser
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: a web link can be applied to the selected text
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: a page link can be applied from the page picker
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: page link search filters matching results and shows a no-result state
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: pressing Escape closes the link picker without creating a link
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: Cancel closes the link picker without changing the selection
inline-toolbar › 11. Inline Text Selection & Formatting Toolbar / Link and slash actions :: the slash button closes the toolbar and opens the slash menu

testcase.name: page-permissions › 32. Page Permissions ABAC / Move modal restrictions :: non-owners keep Duplicate but cannot see private workspaces as move destinations
page-permissions › 32. Page Permissions ABAC / Duplicate fallback :: non-owners can duplicate shared pages into an owned shared copy
page-permissions › 32. Page Permissions ABAC / Owner move rights :: owner can move a shared page into a private workspace and it becomes private there
table-cell-editing › 33. Table Cell Editing / Cell focus :: typing in a cell keeps focus inside that same cell
table-cell-editing › 33. Table Cell Editing / Keyboard movement :: Tab and Shift+Tab move focus to next and previous cells

testcase.name: local-persistence › 29. Local persistence / Seeded offline pages :: edits on an offline seeded page survive refresh and remain editable afterwards
local-persistence › 29. Local persistence / Local pages :: a locally created page survives refresh and can be reopened with its content intact
harness-coverage › 15. Read-only Rendering / Block rendering :: read-only mode renders the main block families correctly
harness-coverage › 15. Read-only Rendering / Hierarchy and containers :: callout and quote children remain rendered inside their read-only containers
harness-coverage › 15. Read-only Rendering / Toggle and numbering :: read-only toggles still expand or collapse and numbered lists reset by context
harness-coverage › 26. Media blocks / Placeholder rendering :: media blocks without an asset show their placeholder preview
